all_results.label: No Boundary Between Symbolic and Sub-symbolic AI
all_results.quote: For me, there is no difference between symbolic AI and sub symbolic AI because I cannot see the boundary between them. The boundary is totally blurred.

all_results.quote: AGI without mastery of the real world is no AGI. AGI without real robots in the real world is no AGI.

all_results.label: Rewards as Task-Defining Inputs
all_results.quote: Standard RL predicts rewards, while UDRL instead uses rewards as task-defining inputs, together with representations of time horizons and other computable functions of historic and desired future data.

all_results.label: RNNs are Essential for AGI
all_results.quote: If we want to build an Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), then its underlying computational substrate must be something more like an RNN than an FNN as FNNs are fundamentally insufficient; RNNs are to FNNs as general computers are to pocket calculators.

all_results.label: Adversarial Artificial Curiosity (GANs)
all_results.quote: It is based on a duel where one neural net minimizes the objective function maximized by another. GANs are simple special cases.

all_results.label: Architecture for a Creative System
all_results.quote: To build a creative system we need just a few crucial ingredients: (1) A predictor or compressor... (2) A learning algorithm... (3) Intrinsic rewards... (4) A separate reward optimizer...

all_results.label: Artificial Curiosity (Adversarial Learning)

all_results.label: Artificial Curiosity (Two-Network Adversarial System)
all_results.quote: the first guy tries to fool the second guy trying to maximize the same objective function that the second network is minimizing... I call it artificial curiosity
